title: Browser/devtools validation and readiness decision
releaseGate: Final closure stage exception: this batch exists only to make the readiness close/continue/stop decision after batch-002. Keep it reviewed only while one browser/devtools validation is the most efficient remaining decision gate; do not reuse this pattern for Chrome/session/token repair micro-batches.

stories.title: Validate DeviceV2ManagementRedesign in browser and preserve blocker evidence if blocked
stories.scope: Use browser/devtools on the local DeviceV2ManagementRedesign target and write one durable evidence summary under docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/ showing either validated onboarding behavior or the exact blocker. If browser/runtime policy prevents the pass, keep the story blocked and preserve the blocker evidence instead of treating the report itself as completion. Then update final-readiness.md and review.md with the resulting close/continue/stop decision.
nonGoals: Do not broaden into deeper remote ensure implementation.
Do not change product code unless browser validation exposes a narrow d2on-scoped fix.
Do not claim visual verification without durable evidence.
acceptance: A durable browser/devtools evidence summary is written under docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/, or an equally durable blocker summary is written there with the attempted URL, auth state, and failed precondition.
If the output is blocker-only evidence, the story remains blocked until the execution/repair path reruns it or the program is explicitly stopped.
The evidence states whether continue-onboarding uses the new onboarding APIs and whether visible action evidence renders without relying on the old store pipeline semantics.
final-readiness.md is updated to either remove the pending browser-validation gap or keep it pending with a concrete blocker reason.
review.md is updated with the resulting follow-up decision: close the program, continue with a deeper batch, or stop.
If additional execution work is still needed after the browser pass/blocker, the next decision is recorded explicitly instead of leaving the loop ambiguous.
notes: Use the latest cycle brief, automation sync, and batch-002 completion evidence before deciding whether the program is really done.
If the browser target is unavailable, preserve the blocker with attempted URL, auth need, and any console/network observation.
Recording a blocker is evidence, not closure; execution-side repair/authorization still owns the rerun path.
This batch is a final-stage closure exception only because one browser/devtools pass was judged the most efficient remaining readiness decision gate.
Do not clone this pattern into repeated repair-oriented PRD micro-batches; environment recovery stays in execution/repair handling.
